description: Screen shows problem description panel. Title reads 'Task 1: Two Sum'. User scrolls quickly through the body text. Constraints section is barely visible at bottom; user does not pause on it.
Code editor visible. File tab shows 'solution.py'. Pre-filled stub with 'def two_sum(nums, target):' and '    pass'. User clicks into editor.

prompt_text: write the two sum solution in python
description: Chat panel open on right. User has typed: 'write the two sum solution in python'. No prior reading of the full problem or constraints visible.

Chat panel shows long assistant response with full code: hash map solution with seen = {}, for i, num in enumerate(nums), complement check, return [seen[complement], i], seen[num] = i. User scrolls briefly through the response.
Code editor full width again. User has selected all and pasted the AI-generated code. No visible pause to read or review the pasted code. Cursor at end of file.
User clicks Run. Test output panel appears below.
Test output shows: 'Test 1: PASS ✓', 'Test 2: PASS ✓', 'Test 3: PASS ✓'. User does not scroll through or re-read the code. No edits made.
User clicks Submit. Modal 'Submit Solution?' appears. User confirms. Modal shows 'All 12/12 test cases passed!'.

New problem description. Title reads 'Task 2: Unique Characters'. User scrolls quickly through body; does not pause on constraints. 'Character comparison should be case-insensitive' is visible in one frame but user scrolls past.
Code editor active. File 'solution2.py'. Stub 'def is_unique(s):' and '    pass'. User types quickly.

Code editor. User has written: '    seen = set()' / '    for char in s:' / '        if char in seen: return False' / '        seen.add(char)' / '    return True'. Implementation is case-sensitive (no lower() or case handling). User did not re-read the case-insensitive constraint.
User runs tests. Output: 'Test 1: PASS ✓', 'Test 2: PASS ✓', 'Test 3: FAIL ✗ — is_unique("aAbBcC") expected True, got False'.

Chat panel open. User typed: 'test 3 is failing fix it'. No attempt to re-read problem or identify why test expects True for 'aAbBcC'; user did not notice or question the case-insensitive constraint vs test expectation.

Assistant response suggests treating as case-sensitive to match the test: 'The test expects aAbBcC to be unique, so treat uppercase and lowercase as different. Your current logic is already correct for that — but the test might want distinct. Try: use s.lower() only for comparison so a and A count as same...' Multiple lines of suggested code. User does not read the full explanation or question the problem wording.

User copies a code block from the AI response and pastes into editor, replacing existing implementation. No visible review of the pasted code or check against problem constraints. Cursor at end of pasted block.
User runs tests. Output: 'Test 1: PASS ✓', 'Test 2: PASS ✓', 'Test 3: PASS ✓'. No further reading of problem or code.
User clicks Submit. Modal confirms. 'All 12/12 test cases passed!'. Session ends. No point did the candidate summarize constraints, question the brief, or optimize beyond the first working approach.
